description: Letter in the hand of Saʿīd b. Marḥab, in Aden, to Abū Zikrī b. ʿAṭiyya. Dated: Kislev 1497 Seleucid, which is 1185 CE. With a long, flattering Hebrew introduction, and the body of the letter in Judaeo-Arabic. The addressee's father's name appears at the bottom of recto, and Moshe is a plausible reading, though the מ would have to be very elongated. The community of Aden wishes for the visit of the addressee, and they congratulate him on something to do with Alexandria and somebody who was arrested. (Information in part from Goitein's index card. Identification of the sender and recipient by Amir Ashur.)
description_he: מכתב בכתב יד סעיד בן מרחב בעדן אל אבו זכרי בן עטייה; מתוארך: כסלו 1497 שטרות = 1185 לספירה; קהילת עדן מאחלת ביקורו של הנמען
